session.user: Run `make lint format ty` from /home/runner/work/erk/erk and report results. Do NOT fix anything.
session.assistant: I'll run the lint, format, and type-checking commands and report the results.

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Run make lint format ty to check code quality

session.tool_result:
Exit code 2 make: *** No rule to make target 'format'. Stop.
uv run ruff check All checks passed!
session.assistant: Let me check what targets are available in the Makefile:
session.tool_result:
1→.PHONY: format-check lint prettier prettier-check ty upgrade-ty test test-erkbot py-fast-ci fast-ci all-ci md-check docs-check docs-validate docs-sync-check docs-fix clean publish fix reinstall-erk-tools docs docs-serve docs-deploy exec-reference-check slackbot 2→ 3→prettier: 4→ prettier --write '**/*.md' --ignore-path .gitignore 5→ 6→prettier-check: 7→ prettier --check '**/*.md' --ignore-path .gitignore 8→ 9→format-check: 10→ uv run ruff format --check 11→ 12→lint: 13→ uv run ruff check 14→ 15→fix: 16→ uv run ruff check --fix --unsafe-fixes 17→ uv run ruff format 18→ prettier --write '**/*.md' --ignore-path .gitignore 19→ 20→ty: 21→ uv run ty check 22→ 23→upgrade-ty: 24→ uv remove ty --group dev && uv add --dev ty 25→ 26→# === Package-specific test targets === 27→ 28→test-erkbot: 29→ cd packages/erkbot && uv run pytest tests/ -x -q 30→
... [154 lines omitted] ...
session.assistant: Now I'll run the correct targets that exist: `lint`, `format-check`, and `ty`:

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Run make lint format-check ty to check code quality

session.tool_result: uv run ruff check All checks passed! uv run ruff format --check 1621 files already formatted uv run ty check All checks passed!
session.assistant:
## Results
All quality checks passed successfully:
- **ruff lint** (make lint): Passed - All checks passed - **ruff format check** (make format-check): Passed - 1621 files already formatted - **ty type check** (make ty): Passed - All checks passed
Note: The Makefile uses `format-check` as the target (not `format`). The `format` target would apply changes, but `format-check` validates formatting without modifications.
